title: HTTP method not allowed for the Tripo3D API endpoint
symptom: When sending a request to the Tripo3D API, the response returns a '405 Method Not Allowed' error. This indicates that the HTTP method used is not supported by the endpoint.
applies_when: Using HTTP node to send requests to the Tripo3D API
not_this_if: Using the correct HTTP method as per API documentation
The endpoint supports the method being used
root_cause: The '405 Method Not Allowed' error indicates that the HTTP method specified in the request (e.g., POST) is not supported by the API endpoint. This can happen if the endpoint is configured to accept only certain methods (like GET) and the request is made with an unsupported method.
fix_steps: Check the API documentation for the correct HTTP method for the endpoint.
Change the HTTP method in the HTTP node to the one specified in the documentation.
agent_instruction: Verify and use the correct HTTP method as specified in the Tripo3D API documentation.
